design_philosophy.core_loop: Build rides and facilities → manage staff and finances → optimize park profitability → expand to new locations worldwide
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing capital investment in attractions against operational costs (staff, maintenance, research) while competing against rivals for annual performance ratings
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Design and operate a thriving amusement park empire, customizing every aspect from ride mechanics to food flavoring to create a profitable business
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Heavily realism-focused simulation that requires active management of logistics, negotiations, stocks, and research rather than passive building; park degradation (ride explosions, staff strikes, vandalism) creates consequence-rich gameplay where neglect has direct, visible impact

difficulty.checkpointing: Calendar-based yearly checkpoints where player is judged on annual performance against rivals
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Three selectable simulation levels (sandbox with no logistics requirements, intermediate, and full with research/negotiations/stocks management); player can switch modes at any time; adjustable game speed allows pacing control

progression.upgrade_system: Research system funds improvements to ride durability, staff efficiency, bus capacity, and unlocks new rides/shops/facilities; player allocates funding to research topics of choice
progression.unlock_mechanism: Research-gated access to 30+ attractions (bouncy castle to roller coaster to Ferris wheel), shops, and facilities; higher simulation levels unlock negotiation and stock management systems
replayability_hooks: Multiple worldwide park locations with different economic conditions, weather, terrain, and land values
Customizable ride and shop attributes (e.g., ice cream sugar content) affecting customer behavior
Trophies awarded for specific achievements (longest roller coaster, etc.)
Cash awards for strong annual performance encourage optimization strategies
Sandbox mode for creative building without management pressure

world_building_techniques: Varied park locations worldwide (UK, desert, glacier settings) with region-specific economic and weather factors
Emergent narrative through visitor behavior responses to park customization and maintenance states
Visual degradation (messy footpaths, broken rides) and vandalism events reflect park management neglect
Staff roles and visitor types create socioeconomic simulation within parks

faction_indicators: Rival parks competing for annual performance rankings
Staff categories (entertainers, security, mechanics, handymen) with distinct roles and strike potential
Visitor demographics responding to park conditions and customization
danger_signals: Thug vandalism events triggered by visitor unhappiness (balloon popping, food theft, entertainer assault)
Staff strikes from wage negotiation failures
Loss of shipments from vendor agreement breakdowns
Messy footpaths and crime from insufficient staff allocation
environmental_storytelling: Park degradation states visually reflect management competence
Customized scenery (trees, fountains) and ride theming create distinct park identities
Show themes (clowns, medieval) provide thematic park branding
Regional settings create geographical context (desert, glacier variants affecting gameplay)
